{{Quote|Sir! That thing's not just coming ''toward'' us! I-it's on a ''collision course! An Imperial probe droid!'' But it's bigger...'' better ''armed!''|A Rebel officer, seeing 13-K's approach|<ref name="marvel45" />}}
'''13-K''' was an [[Infiltrator probe droid|Infiltrator]] [[Probe droid/Legends|probe droid]] produced in a collaboration between [[Arakyd Industries/Legends|Arakyd Industries]] and [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Technician/Legends|technicians]] under [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Damon Krell]] following the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]. Augmented with superior [[Armor/Legends|armor]], weapons and [[Computer/Legends|computer]] interface systems, 13-K became the center of Krell's plot to wipe out the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]]. 13-K commandeered a Rebel [[CR90 corvette/Legends|blockade runner]] and intended to detonate the ship at the [[Haven (rendezvous point)|Rebel rendezvous point]]. However, the meddling of Rebel [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], and a failure of 13-K's [[logic capacitator]]s, led 13-K to instead destroy Krell's [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|It's Probe 13-K, sir. One of the models you had specially augmented by the technological section.|An Imperial comms officer, reporting to Admiral Damon Krell|<ref name="marvel45" />}}
Created in the wake of the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the [[Probe droid/Legends|probe droid]] designated 13-K was created as one of the first [[Infiltrator probe droid]]s, created as part of a joint venture between [[Arakyd Industries/Legends|Arakyd Industries]] and [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Technician/Legends|technicians]] under the supervision of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Damon Krell]]. As part of the Infiltrator program, 13-K was upgraded with additional [[Armor/Legends|armor]] and weapons, plus the ability to plug in and completely control electronic systems.<ref name="negtd" /> Desiring recognition from the [[Palpatine/Legends|Emperor]], Krell saw the Infiltrators as his chance to wipe out the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] in one fell swoop. Krell dispatched 13-K on a solo mission to capture a Rebel [[CR90 corvette/Legends|blockade runner]], which it did without issue, blasting through the [[Starship/Legends|ship's]] [[Hull/Legends|hull]] and [[Death/Legends|killing]] everyone aboard before an alarm could be raised.<ref name="marvel45" />

[[File:InfiltratorProbeDroid.jpg|thumb|left|250px|13-K commandeering a Rebel blockade runner.]]
That was the first stage of Krell's plan for glory—the next step was for 13-K to plug into the blockade runner's systems, commandeer the ship, return to the Rebel [[Haven (rendezvous point)|rendezvous point]] and self-destruct, causing catastrophic damage. However, a chance encounter during the ensuing transit led to the plan's unraveling, when 13-K happened across and destroyed an [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] by Rebel hero [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. Skywalker and his [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] managed to make their way aboard 13-K's ship and determine what was happening, also learning that 13-K's link into the ship's [[Reactor/Legends|reactor]] ensured that attempts to damage or dislodge the droid would result in their deaths.<ref name="marvel45" />

Although Skywalker was in a seemingly impossible situation, the young [[Human/Legends|Human]] was suddenly struck with an idea. Skywalker started causing damage aboard the blockade runner, prompting 13-K to respond by overloading a wall circuit and causing an explosion that knocked Skywalker unconscious. Upon awakening, Skywalker used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to keep his vital signs at coma level, masking his presence from 13-K as he sabotaged key systems within the ship. In the meantime, Skywalker's presence triggered prior programming directly from the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], which ordered it to take Skywalker alive and return him personally to the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]]. 13-K deviated from its previous trajectory towards the Rebel fleet and instead turned back, ignoring Krell's commands to proceed.<ref name="marvel45" />

13-K intended to return to turn Skywalker over to Krell, but all the while, Skywalker's sabotage was turning the blockade runner into a ticking time bomb. Skywalker weakened the ship's systems enough that him and R2-D2 were able to launch an [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]], and with the blockade runner's reactor still wired to explode, 13-K suffered a catastrophic failure in its [[logic capacitator]]s that prevented it from acknowledging Krell's directives. 13-K's ship ultimately exploded and ripped through Krell's [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]], bringing an end to droid and creator both.<ref name="marvel45" />

==Characteristics==
{{Quote|That thing's tapped into ''every'' system... made the entire ship practically an ''extension'' of itself! Someone's ''really'' augmented its programming!|Luke Skywalker|<ref name="marvel45" />}}
Based off the shape of the more well-known [[Viper probe droid/Legends|Viper probe droid]], 13-K had several significant upgrades over its commonplace counterpart, including thick armor plating, augmented weapons and the ability to directly control electronic systems.<ref name="negtd" /> It was much larger than a regular Viper probe droid, and its firepower was such that it could blast a hole clean through the hull of a [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]]. 13-K also had the ability to operate and make decisions much more independently than a typical probe droid, but its augmented systems struggled to integrate with its regular programming, causing a crucial failure of its logic capacitators during its final mission.<ref name="marvel45" />

==Behind the scenes==
13-K first appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 45|''Star Wars'' (1977) 45]], an issue of the original [[Star Wars (1977)|Marvel ''Star Wars'']] comic series, written by [[Archie Goodwin]] and released on [[December 2]], [[1980]]. In the issue, the droid was illustrated by [[Carmine Infantino]].<ref name="marvel45" />
